summary: All 6 acceptance criteria from task 5.md are satisfied. The HealthController implementation is minimal and correct. The justified addition of a third @MockBean (RedisConnectionFactory) to handle Spring Boot 3.5.x RedisAutoConfiguration is a valid correction to the spec, not a deviation from intent. Two minor quality concerns are noted: HealthEndpointTest and HealthSecurityTest are functionally identical, and @MockBean is deprecated in Spring Boot 3.4+.

suggestions.suggestion: HealthSecurityTest.healthPermittedWithoutAuth and HealthEndpointTest.healthEndpointReturns200WithOkBody are functionally identical — they both send GET /health without an Authorization header and assert HTTP 200 + $.status == "ok". The distinction the spec intended is: HealthEndpointTest verifies the controller wiring (endpoint exists, returns correct body), while HealthSecurityTest verifies the Security filter chain permits the request. To make the difference explicit and meaningful, HealthSecurityTest should assert only the HTTP status (not the body), or it should explicitly pass an Authorization header in HealthEndpointTest and omit it in HealthSecurityTest. Currently, if the controller were somehow protected and returned 401, both tests would fail for the same reason, providing no diagnostic signal about whether the failure is a routing or security issue.
suggestions.benefit: Tests that exercise distinct concerns provide clearer failure signals. When HealthSecurityTest fails, the cause is unambiguously the Security filter chain, not controller logic.

suggestions.suggestion: @MockBean is deprecated in Spring Boot 3.4.0 and will be removed in a future release. The replacement is @MockitoBean (org.springframework.test.context.bean.override.mockito.MockitoBean). All three test classes use @MockBean — update all three when migrating. The same applies to HealthSecurityTest.java and SecurityBlockTest.java.
suggestions.benefit: Avoids deprecation warnings in build output and prevents a future breaking change when @MockBean is removed.

suggestions.suggestion: All three test classes (HealthEndpointTest, HealthSecurityTest, SecurityBlockTest) duplicate the same three @MockBean declarations and @Autowired WebTestClient field. Consider extracting a shared abstract base class (e.g., AbstractWebFluxIntegrationTest) annotated with @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = RANDOM_PORT) that declares the three @MockBean fields and the WebTestClient field. Concrete test classes extend it. This eliminates 3x duplication and ensures a future fourth @MockBean (e.g., for a new auto-config) only needs to be added in one place.
suggestions.benefit: Single point of change when the mock set needs updating; reduces copy-paste risk across test files.

acceptanceCriteriaVerification.gradleBuildPasses: VERIFIED — smoke result confirms BUILD SUCCESSFUL, all 3 tests pass
acceptanceCriteriaVerification.healthReturns200WithJsonBody: VERIFIED — Mono<Map<String, String>> with Map.of("status", "ok") serializes to {"status":"ok"} with Content-Type application/json
acceptanceCriteriaVerification.plainRestControllerNoActuator: VERIFIED — @RestController only; spring-boot-starter-actuator absent from build.gradle
acceptanceCriteriaVerification.mockBeanStrategy: VERIFIED WITH JUSTIFIED DEVIATION — spec said 2 @MockBean fields; implementation has 3 (MongoClient, RedisConnectionFactory, ReactiveRedisConnectionFactory). The addition of RedisConnectionFactory is required by Spring Boot 3.5.x RedisAutoConfiguration and is correctly documented in the deviation report.
